questions.question: Write a shell script that sets the current working directory to /home/user and then prints the path of the home directory to the console. Explain the variables used in the script.
A script sets the PATH environment variable to /bin:/usr/bin. What is the effect of this on the PATH variable in the script?
Explain the difference between the $PATH and $HOME environment variables. Provide an example of how you can use them in a shell script.
